svg.text: The Quality Chain
The agent that writes never reviews its own work alone — and the record is mechanical.
spec — [A] Approve & review
⚡ auto-review · SUB-AGENT, fresh context
framing alignment · testable criteria · boundary completeness · edge cases · internal consistency
spec-gate hook: no source edit goes through while the cycle is pre-spec
plan — [A] Approve & review
⚡ auto-review · SUB-AGENT, fresh context
spec-plan alignment · task decomposition · dependency ordering · coverage gaps · risk concentration
implement
coding principles on every line: clarity · error handling · boundary guards · minimal scope · safe APIs
per-task commits · tdd-gate blocks an untested source edit · secrets-gate blocks a hardcoded credential
bookkeeping is one command — hand-edits of manifest/decisions redirect to `manifest.py close-out`
--subagents: a fresh implementer + reviewer per task, tracked in a generated ledger (opt-in)
QUALITY GATES
Deterministic scripts — always run, and run FIRST
Gate 1 spec-check (deliverables) · Gate 4 hallucination (imports, toolchain) · Gate 5 verify (tests)
Gate 6 visual (UI tasks only, browser toolchain)
Exit 0 pass · 1 fail · 2 unverifiable — unverifiable stops and asks, never upgrades to a pass
--quiet: PASS is one line, FAIL never trimmed · resume: targeted test per step, full suite at close-out
Independent judgment — ⚡ ONE combined adversarial review of the whole cycle diff
spec compliance (G1) ∧ constitution (G2) ∧ code quality (G3) ∧ auto-QA (G8) — one sub-agent, fresh context
gate_review: combined (default) · per-gate = a reviewer per gate · off = self-review
The independent whole-change review always happens — it is the part that catches real defects
requires subagent dispatch; without it the skip is recorded mechanically (see below)
✓ done — guarded, not declared
verify-gate: a code-bearing commit without this-session test evidence exits 2 — run the tests, then commit
spec-gate: `complete` is refused while qa: is pending or any ledger task lacks independent approval
[S] Skip review is explicit — and the decisions.md record is written by a hook, not requested from the model
`updated:` and fact states are stamped by machine — approval states never are
config-gate wraps the whole chain: an agent cannot edit away its own enforcement while enforcement is on
Also sub-agent reviewed: ADR (/architect) · root cause & fix plan (/fix) — advisory: [P] Proceed always works
Mechanical on Claude Code and opencode (Tier A). Findings log to decisions.md for /reflect to learn from.
